Steps: 1. Check: SELECT tablename, rowsecurity FROM pg_tables WHERE schemaname='public'
Expected Result:
RLS enabled on sensitive tables:
- users (rowsecurity = true)
- org_subscriptions (true)
- registration_requests (true)
- company_registration_requests (true)

Test Name: RLS Policy - Anon Can Insert Registration
Steps:
1. Use anon key to insert into registration_requests
2. Try to SELECT (should fail)
Expected Result:
1. INSERT succeeds (anon can submit)
2. SELECT fails (anon cannot read)
3. Only authenticated super_duper_admin can read

Test Name: RLS Policy - User Can Read Own Data
Steps:
1. Login as user
2. Query: SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = auth.uid()
Expected Result:
1. Can read own user record
2. Cannot read other users (RLS blocks)
3. Can read own org subscription

- role = default (if set)
- account_type = default
- status = 'pending' (for requests)
